key_insights.insight: Communication skills are crucial for negotiating reality and achieving desired outcomes in life.
key_insights.quote: Reality is negotiable, cool. Well, what skill do we use to negotiate the reality we desire? It's our ability to communicate.

key_insights.insight: Technical skills alone are insufficient; pairing them with strong communication skills is key to thriving.
key_insights.quote: When you pair technical skills with great communication skills, that's when you thrive, I believe.

key_insights.insight: People tend to stick with the vocal habits they developed early in life, unaware that they can consciously change their communication style.
key_insights.quote: But the way they communicate and the way they sound stays consistent generally. It's because there are a lot of psychological blocks that keep us sounding the same.

key_insights.insight: The voice people think is 'natural' is often a series of learned behaviors and imitations picked up from others.
key_insights.quote: The voice that you've developed, you've copied that based on the people you were inspired by when you were young. It's just a series of habits.

key_insights.insight: Three simple changes – increasing volume, adding melody, and using larger hand gestures – can profoundly alter how others perceive you.
key_insights.quote: More volume, more melody, bigger hand gestures, people completely change the way they perceive them. Three simple things that they do creates a profound change in how others perceive them.

key_insights.insight: There are five core vocal foundations: melody, rate of speech, volume, tonality (emotion), and pause, each contributing to clarity and impact.
key_insights.quote: Volume carries all the other foundations you're about to learn. Volume carries the melody. Volume carries the rate of speech.

key_insights.insight: To add more emotion into your voice, one should try moving their face and making different faces to reflect different emotions.
key_insights.quote: The way to add more emotion into your voice is to move your face. Because as I eloquently put it at times, your face is the remote control that allows you to add emotion into your voice.

key_insights.insight: Effective storytelling involves reliving the story for the audience by incorporating visual, auditory, kinesthetic, and sensory details (VAKS).
key_insights.quote: When you think of the storytelling formula, if you just give the who, what, where and when, that's the basics people need. But what turns it from reporting to reliving… is the ingredients that you just called out. VAKS, which stands for visual, auditory, kinesthetic, and smell.

key_insights.insight: Consistent, focused effort on even small communication improvements (kaizen) can lead to significant long-term change.
key_insights.quote: And I love this Japanese word called kaizen, relentless improvement. That's what you've got to commit to.

key_insights.insight: On Zoom, being generous with energy is important and it is crucial to light yourself well and use an external camera to come across as visually dynamic.
key_insights.quote: But when you have to do it virtually, you have to give more of yourself. If you want them to feel valued, you have to adopt a mindset of generosity... push the laptop back, external camera, wider lens, let them see your whole torso.

actionable_advice.advice: Practice 'Record and Review' to assess your communication style.
actionable_advice.how_to: Record yourself speaking impromptu for 5 minutes, review the video for auditory, visual, and transcribed text elements to identify areas for improvement.

actionable_advice.advice: Use the 'Siren Technique' to expand vocal range and add melody to your voice.
actionable_advice.how_to: Read text while gradually shifting from a low voice to a high voice (even falsetto) and back down, experimenting with pitch variations within each sentence.

actionable_advice.advice: Increase self-awareness by consciously choosing and practicing different hand gestures (placata, leveler, blamer, computer, distractor)
actionable_advice.how_to: Learn some gestures and try using those, in your everyday conversations
Get an external camera, set up the lighting as three-point-lighting to be crisper, and use an external microphone to sound better

actionable_advice.advice: Prime conversations before you start playing your instrument
actionable_advice.how_to: Before a conversation in which you will be applying new vocal foundations ask them if they're ok with that

memorable_stories: The story of Miss Stanley, the vocal teacher, who taught the guest the power of the voice through piano.
The guest's experience being bullied and called 'FOB' due to his accent, and how it motivated him.
Guest's account of how his son, diagnosed with autism, overcame communication challenges through gradual exposure and his own teaching.
Guest's parent's leaving their wealth to become Buddhist monks, focusing on simple life
contrarian_claims: The voice you have is not your natural voice, but a set of learned habits.
Accents are not the problem, articulation and pronunciation are.
Accents are not the problem, and that people in those communities are discriminated upon
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Public Speaking Expert: This Speaking Mistake Makes People Ignore You! These Small Mistakes Make You Unlikeable!
